summary: Small multi-file decomp matching PR touching fighter Kirby, game-mode, result-screen, stage, interface, and snapshot code. Bot report records +940 matched-code bytes, 6 new 100% matches, and 2 unmatched-item improvements. The diff includes signature/prototype corrections, callsite argument reordering, assert macro cleanup, pointer-lifetime/register-shape steering, and narrow PAD_STACK/local temporary tactics.

key_files.role: Changed ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 return type to u8*, widened pointer lifetime for p, and returned p after nested FtPartsVisLookup iteration.
Updated ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 prototype from void to u8* to match recovered body signature.
Adjusted gm_801674C4 local temporaries, casts, pointer aliases, and PAD_STACK usage; bot reports the function reached 100%.
Changed fn_8018E618 signature parameter order from int,int,f32 to int,f32,int and updated multiple callsites; bot reports fn_8019B860 as a new match and fn_8019B458 as improved.
Updated fn_8018E618 header prototype to match the recovered argument order.
Changed fn_80174920 return type to u8 and reshaped fn_801749B8 locals, pointer access, casts, and GXSetScissor arithmetic; bot reports both functions as new matches.
Updated fn_80174920 prototype from void to u8.

Included baselib/debug.h and it/inlines.h, introduced GET_ITEM2 inline, renamed gobj to map_gobj, replaced raw __assert pattern with HSD_ASSERT, and used temporary alias/PAD_STACK for fn_801FBF6C; bot reports the function as a new match.

Adjusted un_802FF620 loop body by removing PAD_STACK/self-assignment, adding a typed pointer temporary to base[i].x8, and documenting similarity with un_802FF570; bot reports unmatched-item improvement.
Introduced a temporary pointer to lbSnap_803BACC8 so the address passed to lb_8001C4A8 is taken through tmp->x14.

what_changed: Updated recovered function signatures and matching headers for ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420, fn_8018E618, and fn_80174920.
Reordered fn_8018E618 arguments and updated all visible callsites in gm_18A5.c from int,int,float-style ordering to int,float,int ordering.
Converted a grinishie1.c raw assert pattern around map_gobj into HSD_ASSERT(0x425, map_gobj), with baselib/debug.h included.
Used local aliasing, declaration movement, casts, and pointer lifetime changes to influence MWCC codegen in several functions.
Bot report records 6 new matches: fn_80174920, fn_801749B8, gm_801674C4, fn_801FBF6C, fn_8019B860, and ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420.
Bot report records 2 improvements in unmatched items: fn_8019B458 and un_802FF620.

smart_moves.text: Header prototypes were updated alongside recovered body signatures, avoiding stale declarations after signature changes.
The grinishie1.c assert cleanup follows the project pattern of replacing direct __assert blocks with HSD_ASSERT when the line number and expression are known.
The PR kept several changes narrow to single functions or direct callsites rather than broad subsystem rewrites.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered body proves a non-void return or different argument order, update the owning header and all visible callsites in the same patch.
Assert macro cleanup can be part of matching work when the raw __assert file, line, and expression map directly to HSD_ASSERT.

Pointer aliasing and moving temporary declarations can be effective narrow matching tactics, but the PR evidence shows they were score-driven and should not be generalized as preferred style without objdiff/build evidence.

naming_conventions.name: map_gobj
naming_conventions.context: Used in grinishie1.c for the Ground_801C2BA4(3) result and the HSD_ASSERT expression.

naming_conventions.name: ip
naming_conventions.context: Local Item* role name remains short and type-indicative after changing access through GET_ITEM2.

naming_conventions.name: new_var/new_var2/new_var3/new_var4/tmp/thing
naming_conventions.context: Several temporary names are matching-oriented and not evidence-backed semantic names; do not treat them as naming conventions.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Widen local pointer lifetime and return the final pointer value to satisfy ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 codegen.
Change function signature argument ordering and update all callsites to align integer and float argument passing for fn_8018E618.
Use pointer aliases, explicit casts, and PAD_STACK in gm_801674C4 and fn_801FBF6C; standards require such tactics to remain narrow and evidence-backed.
Use typed pointer temporaries to alter address-taking and field access shape, as in ifcoget.c and lbsnap.c.
Use HSD_ASSERT instead of an expanded __assert block where the source file, line, and expression are known.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.comment: Report for GALE01 records +0.02% matched code, +940 bytes, 6 new matches, and 2 improvements in unmatched items.

follow_up_queries: fn_8018E618 argument order int float int callsite
gmresult fn_80174920 return clearly wrong but match is match
un_802FF620 same issue as un_802FF570 typed pointer temporary

notes: Full diff and changed-file metadata are loaded and support file-level change extraction.
PR body is empty, so author intent beyond title and diff must not be inferred.
Bot report provides matching-score evidence for specific functions but not detailed objdiff listings or adjacent regression checks.
Several changes are explicitly matching-shaped or use weak semantic names; they are recorded as tactics, not accepted source style.
Review record is present but one review state is DISMISSED; only the visible inline comment is treated as feedback evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Synchronize recovered signatures with headers and callsites
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2639 shows signature recovery landing with matching header/callsite updates for ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420, fn_8018E618, and fn_80174920.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use assert macros for identifiable raw assert expansions
accepted_candidate_records.text: In grinishie1.c, a raw __assert pattern tied to grinishie1.c line 0x425 and map_gobj was replaced with HSD_ASSERT(0x425, map_gobj), matching reviewer feedback.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Narrow local temporary and alias steering can close low-hanging matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2639 used declaration movement, aliases, casts, pointer temporaries, and PAD_STACK narrowly across several functions; bot report shows associated matches/improvements, but these remain tactics requiring verification rather than global style.

source_update_candidates.title: Reinforce HSD_ASSERT cleanup when raw assert file/line/expression are known
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2639 provides review-backed evidence that direct __assert("grinishie1.c", 0x425, "map_gobj")-style blocks should be considered for HSD_ASSERT(0x425, map_gobj) when the macro form preserves file/line/expression behavior.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is consistent with existing standards and has explicit review evidence in the PR slice; curator may decide whether it is worth adding as an example.

source_update_candidates.title: Document that score-closing signature changes can expose suspicious but matched returns
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2639 changed fn_80174920 from void to u8 with the inline note 'Return clearly wrong but match is match'. This should not be promoted as style, but may warrant a cautionary example under truthful headers or matching-tactics standards: suspicious recovered returns need verification and should be documented as match-driven.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR evidence supports the existence of a match-driven suspicious return-type change, but not enough to make a new accepted global rule without curator review.

rejection_notes: Do not promote new_var/new_var2/new_var3/new_var4, thing, or tmp as naming conventions; they appear to be codegen steering or temporary placeholders.
Do not infer that PAD_STACK, comma-expression address shaping, or suspicious non-void returns are accepted style; standards require narrow verifier evidence.
Do not claim all changed functions are fully matched from the diff alone; only functions named in the bot report are supported as new matches or improvements.
Do not infer reviewer approval or merge rationale beyond the visible merged metadata, bot report, and single inline comment.
